info.title: anony-mail API
info.summary: REST + Server-Sent Events API for an inbound-only disposable email backend.
info.description:
anony-mail is an inbound-only disposable email (temp mail) backend. It receives mail over SMTP for throwaway addresses and exposes it through this HTTP API. It does **not** send mail.

### Conventions
- **Base path:** every resource endpoint lives under `/api`; the health check is at `/healthz`.
- **Content type:** request and response bodies are JSON, except attachment downloads (raw bytes) and the events endpoint (an SSE stream).
- **Addresses are case-insensitive.** They are lowercased server-side before lookup or creation, so `Foo@Example.com` and `foo@example.com` refer to the same mailbox.
- **Timestamps** are UTC in RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 format (e.g. `2026-07-01T11:23:45.123456Z`).
- **IDs** (message and attachment) are UUIDs (v4).

### Errors
On failure the API returns the matching HTTP status code with a JSON body of the form `{ "error": "message" }`. A `500` always reports a generic `"internal server error"` message; details are logged server-side.

### Authentication
There is **no authentication** in v1: anyone who can reach the API can create addresses and read any mailbox's mail. Put it behind your own auth/proxy if you need access control, and restrict browser access with the `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` setting.

### Mailbox lifecycle
Mailboxes expire after `DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS` (default 1 hour) and are periodically purged along with their messages and attachments. Use `POST /api/addresses/{address}/extend` to push the expiry back.

host.description: Hostname where your anony-mail instance is reachable.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Discover which domains this server accepts mail for.

tags.name: Mailboxes
tags.description: Create, inspect, extend, and delete disposable addresses.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: List and read received messages, and download attachments.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Live push notifications of newly arrived mail via Server-Sent Events.

get.description: Simple liveness probe. Always returns `200` with `{ "status": "ok" }` when the HTTP server is up.
Returns the domains this server accepts mail for (the `DOMAINS` setting). The first entry is the default used when creating an address without specifying a domain.

post.description:
Creates a new mailbox and returns it. The request body is **optional**:

- Omit the body entirely (or send `{}`) to get a random 10-character local part on the first configured domain.
- Provide `domain` to choose among the configured domains.
- Provide `local_part` to request a custom address. It must be 1–64 characters of `[a-z0-9._-]` and must not start or end with a separator (`.`, `_`, `-`). If the address is already taken, the request fails with `409 Conflict`.

The new mailbox expires after the server's default TTL (`DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS`, 1 hour by default).

random.summary: Random address on the default domain (empty body)

domainOnly.summary: Random local part on a chosen domain

custom.summary: Custom local part on a chosen domain

409.description: The requested custom address already exists.

get.description: Returns the mailbox's metadata if it exists. Note that an expired-but-not-yet-purged mailbox is still returned here; compare `expires_at` against the current time to determine whether it is still active.

delete.description: Deletes the mailbox and all of its messages and attachments (cascading). Returns `204 No Content` on success.

post.description: Pushes the mailbox's `expires_at` to now + the server default TTL (`DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS`). The extension is relative to the current time, not to the previous expiry. No request body is required.

200.description: Expiry extended; the updated mailbox is returned.

get.description: Returns lightweight summaries of the mailbox's messages, newest first. Bodies and attachment metadata are omitted here — fetch a single message for the full content. An existing mailbox with no mail returns `200` with an empty array `[]`; a missing mailbox returns `404`.

Returns a single message including its text and/or HTML bodies and attachment metadata. Attachment bytes are not inlined — download them individually via the attachments endpoint using each attachment's `id`.

value.subject: Invoice #1042
value.text_body: Thanks for your order. Your invoice is attached.
value.html_body: Thanks for your order. Your invoice is attached.

value.subject: Your verification code
value.text_body: Your verification code is 123456. It expires in 10 minutes.

delete.description: Deletes one message (and its attachments) from the mailbox. Returns `204 No Content` on success.

get.description: Streams the raw bytes of an attachment. The response's `Content-Type` is the attachment's stored MIME type (falling back to `application/octet-stream` if it is not a valid header value), and `Content-Disposition` is set to `attachment` with a sanitized filename to prompt a download.

Content-Type.description: The attachment's stored MIME type, e.g. `application/pdf`.

Content-Disposition.description: Download disposition with a sanitized ASCII filename.

404.description: The attachment (or its message/mailbox) was not found.

200.description: An open `text/event-stream`. Each `message` event carries a JSON `MailEvent` in its `data` field.

schema.description:
SSE-framed stream. Each record is `event: message
data: <MailEvent JSON>

`. See the MailEvent schema for the data shape.

AddressPath.name: address
AddressPath.description: Full email address of the mailbox. Case-insensitive (lowercased server-side). The `@` may be sent literally or percent-encoded as `%40`.

BadRequest.description: The request was invalid (e.g. unknown domain or malformed `local_part`).

unknownDomain.summary: Domain is not in the server's DOMAINS list

value.error: local_part must be 1-64 chars of a-z, 0-9, '.', '_' or '-' and not start/end with a separator

MailboxNotFound.description: No mailbox exists for this address.

MessageNotFound.description: No such message in this mailbox.

InternalError.description: An unexpected server error occurred. Details are logged server-side.

CreateAddressRequest.description: Options for creating a mailbox. Every field is optional; an empty object (or no body) yields a random address on the first configured domain. Unknown fields are ignored.

local_part.description: Custom local part (the portion before `@`). Must be 1–64 characters of `[a-z0-9._-]` and must not start or end with a separator (`.`, `_`, `-`). Lowercased before use. If omitted, a random 10-character local part is generated.

domain.description: One of the server's configured domains (see `GET /api/domains`). Defaults to the first configured domain when omitted.

address.description: Full email address of the mailbox (lowercased).

expires_at.description: When the mailbox expires and becomes eligible for purging (UTC).

MessageSummary.description: Lightweight message representation used in inbox listings (no bodies or attachment metadata).

mail_from.description: SMTP envelope sender (MAIL FROM) of the message.

subject.description: Message subject, or null if the email had no Subject header.

received_at.description: When the server received and stored the message (UTC).

has_attachments.description: True if the message has one or more attachments.

AttachmentMeta.description: Attachment metadata returned alongside a full message. Download the bytes via the attachment endpoint.

id.description: Attachment identifier; use it in the download URL.

filename.description: Original filename, or null if the part had none.

content_type.description: MIME type of the attachment as parsed from the message.

size.description: Size of the attachment content in bytes.

StoredMessage.description: A fully materialised message including bodies and attachment metadata.

mailbox_address.description: Address of the mailbox this message belongs to.

mail_from.description: SMTP envelope sender (MAIL FROM) of the message.

subject.description: Message subject, or null if absent.

message_date.description: Date parsed from the message's Date header (UTC), or null if missing/unparseable.

text_body.description: Plain-text body, or null if the message had none.
examples: Thanks for your order. Your invoice is attached.

html_body.description: HTML body, or null if the message had none.
examples: Thanks for your order. Your invoice is attached.

raw_size.description: Size in bytes of the raw message as received over SMTP (DATA phase).

received_at.description: When the server received and stored the message (UTC).

attachments.description: Attachment metadata (bytes downloaded separately). Empty when there are none.

MailEvent.description: Payload delivered in the `data` field of each SSE `message` event. Mirrors a MessageSummary plus the target `address`, giving enough to render an inbox row immediately; treat the REST listing as authoritative.

address.description: The mailbox address the message arrived for.

id.description: Identifier of the newly stored message.

mail_from.description: SMTP envelope sender (MAIL FROM) of the message.

subject.description: Message subject, or null if absent.

received_at.description: When the server received and stored the message (UTC).

has_attachments.description: True if the message has one or more attachments.

DomainsResponse.description: The set of domains the server accepts mail for.

domains.description: Configured domains; the first is the default for address creation.

status.description: Always `"ok"` when the server is healthy.

Error.description: Uniform error envelope returned on any non-2xx response.
